label: Stress Scenarios
description: Historical stress scenario table with multiplier, estimated P&L impact, breach count, and on-demand stress test action.

0.4.note: No any/ts-ignore; as unknown as StressRow[] is a legitimate typed cast (context types Array<Record<string,unknown>>).

0.6.note: isLoading passed to TableWidget which renders skeletons.

0.7.note: emptyMessage='No stress scenarios available' in TableWidget.

0.8.note: error={hasError ? 'Failed to load stress scenarios' : null} passed to TableWidget.

l0.notes: 0.1 WidgetErrorBoundary in widget-wrapper.tsx. 0.2 requiredEntitlements=[execution-basic,execution-full]. 0.3 availableOn=[risk]. 0.4 No any/ts-ignore; as unknown as StressRow[] is a legitimate typed cast (context types Array<Record<string,unknown>>). 0.5 No lib/mocks imports. 0.6 isLoading passed to TableWidget which renders skeletons. 0.7 emptyMessage='No stress scenarios available' in TableWidget. 0.8 error={hasError ? 'Failed to load stress scenarios' : null} passed to TableWidget. 0.9 No dead code.

1.1.note: Data via useRiskData() hook; stressScenarios/regimeData/stressTestResult all from context.

1.2.note: use-risk-page-data.ts calls useGlobalScope(). 1.3-1.5 stressScenarios sourced from useStressScenarios() API hook in context; not directly entity-filtered (stress scenarios are portfolio-level, not per-strategy) — appropriate.

1.3.note: 1.5 stressScenarios sourced from useStressScenarios() API hook in context; not directly entity-filtered (stress scenarios are portfolio-level, not per-strategy) — appropriate.

1.5.note: stressScenarios sourced from useStressScenarios() API hook in context; not directly entity-filtered (stress scenarios are portfolio-level, not per-strategy) — appropriate.

1.6.note: — stress scenarios are not scope-filtered (correct: portfolio-level data).

1.7.note: No mock constants in widget. regimeMultiplier slider and scenario selector are wired to context setters (setRegimeMultiplier, setSelectedStressScenario).

l1.notes: 1.1 Data via useRiskData() hook; stressScenarios/regimeData/stressTestResult all from context. 1.2 use-risk-page-data.ts calls useGlobalScope(). 1.3-1.5 stressScenarios sourced from useStressScenarios() API hook in context; not directly entity-filtered (stress scenarios are portfolio-level, not per-strategy) — appropriate. 1.6 N/A — stress scenarios are not scope-filtered (correct: portfolio-level data). 1.7 No mock constants in widget. regimeMultiplier slider and scenario selector are wired to context setters (setRegimeMultiplier, setSelectedStressScenario).

l2.notes: Covered globally by commits 32ba8ab and f9f29ea. Re-audit only if wiring test fails or entitlements change.

3.1.note: Applies to: live ✓ (real-time stress), paper ✓, batch ✓ (historical scenarios). Stress multiplier slider and scenario select work in all modes.

3.2.note: isBatchMode available in context; not directly used by this widget (stress scenarios are analysis tools, not execution).

3.5.note: Paper mode: stress scenarios still valid for analysis; no order execution in widget.

3.6.note: No WebSocket/interval in widget. Stress test result via SWR hook on selectedStressScenario change.

l3.notes: Human must verify 3.4 (batch indicator visible in layout).

4.1.note: Stress multiplier input calls setRegimeMultiplier. Scenario select calls setSelectedStressScenario. Both are real state handlers.

4.6.note: No entitlement checks in render path.

l4.notes: Human must verify 4.4 (scenario select triggers stress test result display), 4.7 (N/A), 4.8 (table scrolls within widget).

5.1.note: Target strategies: all risk-bearing strategies. Widget is analysis tool — applies stress scenarios across all positions.

7.1.note: No hardcoded hex colors in widget.

7.5.note: Out of scope — English-only UI per product decision 2026-04-21; 7.5 reassigned to UTC datetime rendering

7.7.note: columns array defined at module level (stable ref). No inline arrays in deps.

8.1.note: text-[10px] replaced with text-micro tokens per consolidated globals.css; no arbitrary typography classes remain.

8.2.note: No bracket spacing violations. w-[160px] in SelectTrigger is an allowlisted fixed width, not spacing.

8.3.note: Uses Badge, Select, Skeleton, TableWidget from shared/ui/*.

8.4.note: Added aria-label='Stress multiplier' to range input.

8.5.note: Human: resize test at minW=6, minH=3 required.

l8.notes: 8.1 fixed (tokens applied). 8.2/8.3 pass. 8.4 fixed (aria-label added). 8.5 needs human verification.

keepSeparate.rationale: Scenario stress testing is distinct from limit/exposure tables.

relationships.notes: On-demand stress test result shown in widget header above table. regime multiplier shared with risk context.

dataContract.notes: stressScenarios from useStressScenarios(). On-demand stress test via useStressTest(selectedStressScenario). regimeData from useRegime().

gaps: Only 3 scenario options wired (GFC_2008, COVID_2020, CRYPTO_BLACK_THURSDAY); custom scenarios not supported
coverage.notes: Analysis widget applicable to all asset classes.

findings.description: No fixes required — widget was already L0/L1 compliant prior to this audit
risk-stress-table-widget.tsx — ~20 user-visible strings (scenario names, column headers, regime labels)
findings.fix: Retired 7.5 i18n check: English-only UI per product decision 2026-04-21
Applied text-micro (10px), text-nano (9px), text-caption (11px) tokens per consolidated globals.css; arbitrary text-[Npx] classes removed
Added aria-label='Stress multiplier' to range input

findings.description: risk-stress-table-widget.tsx:121-123 — the on-demand stress test dropdown has 3 hardcoded scenario IDs (GFC_2008, COVID_2020, CRYPTO_BLACK_THURSDAY). Should source from /api/risk/stress-test/scenarios (or a config constant) so the set can evolve without an edit to the widget.
findings.deferReason: Values match the mock API shape; should be replaced when backend returns scenario catalog. Tracked as shared finding for BP-5.
